headline: US airlines hit turbulence with TSA delays, fuel cost pressures
summary: A partial government shutdown has led to TSA (Transportation Security Administration) slowdowns at major US airports, while the Financial Times reports that airline operators could end up paying an additional $11 billion due to rising jet fuel prices. Morning Brief Host Julie Hyman and Yahoo Finance Head of News Myles Udland comment on the additional pressures that the escalating conflicts in the Middle East are having on oil prices (CL=F, BZ=F) and fuel costs for the broader airline industry. To watch more expert insights and analysis on the latest market action, check out more Morning Brief.

headline: United Airlines Just Had Its Best Ever Day of Bookings. There’s Hope Amid Fuel Fears.
summary: Airline stocks have been crushed since the Iran war started. But it may not be quite as bad as feared for U.S. carriers.
